text: Sm(1) is bonded to two equivalent O(1) and four equivalent O(2) atoms to form HfO6 octahedra that share corners with six equivalent Sm(1)O6 octahedra and corners with eight equivalent Sr(1)O4 tetrahedra. Sr(1) is bonded to two equivalent O(1) and two equivalent O(2) atoms to form distorted CoO4 tetrahedra that share corners with eight equivalent Sm(1)O6 octahedra and corners with two equivalent Sr(1)O4 tetrahedra. The corner-sharing octahedral tilt angles range from 65-82°. There is one shorter (2.07 Å) and one longer (2.18 Å) Sr(1)-O(1) bond length. Both Sr(1)-O(2) bond lengths are 2.03 Å. There are two inequivalent O sites. In the first O site, O(1) is bonded to two equivalent Sm(1) and two equivalent Sr(1) atoms to form distorted corner-sharing OHf2Co2 tetrahedra. In the second O site, O(2) is bonded in a distorted trigonal planar geometry to two equivalent Sm(1) and one Sr(1) atom.
